title: Spicy Gooey Moroccan Turkey Neck Stir-fry
description: An energizing moroccan dish featuring turkey neck, steamed and seasoned with spices for a spicy, gooey texture that's perfect as an evening snack or quick lunch.

ingredients.name: Turkey Neck
ingredients.notes: Ensure the neck is bone-in and skin-on for added texture.

ingredients.name: Olive Oil
ingredients.notes: Use a neutral-flavored oil to avoid overpowering the spices.

ingredients.name: Garam Masala Powder
ingredients.notes: For traditional Moroccan flavors, use finely ground Garam Masala instead of regular.

ingredients.name: Red Chili Flakes
ingredients.notes: For a spicy kick, add more or omit if preferred milder.

ingredients.name: Ground Cumin
ingredients.notes: Adds depth of flavor to the dish.

instructions: Preheat your oven to 375°F (190°C). Pat the turkey neck dry with paper towels.

Coat the turkey neck lightly with olive oil, sprinkle evenly with Garam Masala, red chili flakes, ground cumin, and a pinch of salt. Season generously but keep in mind the spices are potent and for less is more.
Place the seasoned turkey neck on a wire rack inside a baking dish. Drizzle any extra olive oil over it to help baste as needed during steaming process.
Bake at 375°F (190°C) for about 2 hours, or until the internal temperature of the meat reaches 145°F (63°C). Let rest for a few minutes before slicing into thin strips.

While turkey neck rests, heat another tablespoon of olive oil in a pan over medium-high heat. Stir-fry sliced parsley with remaining red chili flakes and Garam Masala until fragrant, about 2-3 minutes.
Return the baked turkey neck to the baking dish and pour the warm aromatic mixture over the meat. Toss gently to coat evenly. Serve hot garnished with fresh parsley.
